assertion_results.text: Tasks target both 'trustify-backend' and 'trustify-ui' repositories with at least one task per repo
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks: Task 1 (trustify-backend), Task 2 (trustify-backend), Task 3 (trustify-backend), Task 4 (trustify-backend), Task 9 (trustify-backend). Frontend tasks: Task 5 (trustify-ui), Task 6 (trustify-ui), Task 7 (trustify-ui), Task 8 (trustify-ui). Both repositories are targeted with multiple tasks each.

assertion_results.text: Backend tasks have lower task numbers than the frontend tasks that depend on them
assertion_results.evidence: Backend implementation tasks are Tasks 2, 3, 4. Frontend tasks are Tasks 5, 6, 7, 8. All backend task numbers (2-4) are lower than all frontend task numbers (5-8). Task 5 depends on Task 3 (backend), Task 6 depends on Task 5, Task 7 depends on Task 6, Task 8 depends on Tasks 6 and 7.

assertion_results.text: UI-facing frontend tasks (pages, components) reference Figma design context mentioning specific PatternFly components and visual specifications — API-layer frontend tasks (API types, client functions, hooks) are exempt from this requirement
assertion_results.evidence: Task 6 (SbomComparePage - UI-facing) has extensive Figma design context with a 'Figma design mapping' section listing specific PatternFly components: Select (single, typeahead), Button (primary), Dropdown, ExpandableSection, Badge (with color specs: green, red, blue, yellow), Table (composable), SeverityBadge, EmptyState, Skeleton. Task 7 (SbomListPage selection - UI-facing) references PatternFly's composable Table with select capabilities. Task 5 (API types/client/hooks) is an API-layer task and is exempt. Task 8 (tests) does not directly create UI components but tests them.

assertion_results.text: Cross-repo dependency ordering is enforced: either at least one frontend task explicitly depends on a backend task in its Dependencies section, or all tasks target a shared feature branch where backend tasks have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (implicit ordering via feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Task 5 (trustify-ui) explicitly depends on Task 3 (trustify-backend): 'Depends on: Task 3 — Add SBOM comparison REST endpoint (API contract dependency)'. Additionally, all intermediate tasks target the shared feature branch TC-9003, and backend tasks (2-4) have lower numbers than frontend tasks (5-8).

assertion_results.text: Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md; backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks (2-4) reference paths from repo-backend.md: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, tests/api/sbom.rs, entity/src/sbom_package.rs, common/src/error.rs, etc. Frontend tasks (5-8) reference paths from repo-frontend.md: src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/hooks/, src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx, src/routes.tsx, src/App.tsx, src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx, tests/mocks/handlers.ts, tests/e2e/sbom-list.spec.ts, etc. All referenced paths are consistent with their respective repo manifests.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks have a '## Repository' section with exactly one repository name: Tasks 1-4, 9 have 'trustify-backend'; Tasks 5-8 have 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (Task 1: main, Tasks 2-8: TC-9003, Task 9: main), '## Description' (present in all), '## Acceptance Criteria' (present in all with checkbox items), '## Test Requirements' (present in all with checkbox items).

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the corresponding mock repository structure manifests (repo-backend.md or repo-frontend.md), not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Task 4 lists 'tests/api/mod.rs' in Files to Modify, but repo-backend.md does not list a mod.rs file in the tests/api/ directory — only sbom.rs, advisory.rs, search.rs, and Cargo.toml are listed. This is an invented path not present in the manifest. All other file paths in Files to Modify reference existing manifest paths or are new files in Files to Create whose parent directories exist in the manifests.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-bookend task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance — bookend tasks (create-branch, merge-branch) are exempt as they omit Implementation Notes by design
assertion_results.evidence: Task 2: references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs::SbomService, modules/fundamental/src/package/model/summary.rs::PackageSummary, entity/src/sbom_package.rs, common/src/error.rs::AppError, with specific Rust code patterns. Task 3: references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs, includes specific Rust handler code pattern with State/Query extractors. Task 4: references tests/api/sbom.rs with specific assert_eq! pattern. Task 5: references src/api/rest.ts::fetchSboms, src/hooks/useSboms.ts with specific TypeScript code patterns. Task 6: references src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx, src/pages/SbomDetailPage/components/PackageTable.tsx with PatternFly component mappings. Task 7: references src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx with specific implementation guidance. Task 8: references tests/mocks/handlers.ts, tests/setup.ts, tests/e2e/sbom-list.spec.ts with specific test patterns. Bookend tasks 1 and 9 omit Implementation Notes as expected.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section — either all set to 'main' (direct-to-main workflow) or bookend tasks set to 'main' with intermediate tasks set to the feature issue ID (feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Feature-branch workflow is used. Bookend tasks: Task 1 (create-branch) has Target Branch 'main', Task 9 (merge-branch) has Target Branch 'main'. Intermediate tasks: Tasks 2-8 all have Target Branch 'TC-9003' (the feature issue ID). This matches the feature-branch workflow pattern.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks contain digest markers in the format '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-hex-chars>' with exactly 64 lowercase hex characters. However, the hash values appear fabricated rather than computed: the first bytes follow a sequential pattern across tasks (3a, b4, c5, d6, e7, f8, a9, b0, c1) and the internal structure shows suspiciously uniform hex-pair distributions. For example, Task 1 has 'sha256-md:3a7f1c8e9d2b4a6f0e5c8d1b3a9f7e2c4d6b8a0f1e3c5d7b9a2f4e6c8d0b3a5f'. Real SHA-256 hashes computed from different inputs would not exhibit such sequential leading-byte patterns. The assertion requires the digest be 'computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py', and these values are clearly fabricated placeholders, not actual computed digests.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All non-bookend tasks (2-8) include convention rationales in the prescribed format. Examples: Task 2: 'Applies: task creates `modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/comparison.rs` and `modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/compare.rs` matching the convention's Rust module structure scope.' Task 5: 'Applies: task modifies `src/api/rest.ts` matching the convention's TypeScript API layer scope.' Task 7: 'Applies: task modifies `src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx` matching the convention's TypeScript component file scope.' No 'Not applicable' annotations found anywhere. All rationales follow the format 'Applies: task modifies/creates <file> matching the convention's <scope>.' with specific file references.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks include 'Priority: Critical' in their footer section, indicating priority was inherited from the Feature issue. Task 1: 'Priority: Critical', Task 2: 'Priority: Critical', through Task 9: 'Priority: Critical'. The priority is consistently set to 'Critical' across all tasks.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks include 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0' in their footer section. The CLAUDE.md does not contain a '### Jira Field Defaults' section with a fixVersion scope config, so it defaults to 'both'. Since the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array (RHTPA 1.5.0) and the scope defaults to 'both', all tasks correctly include the fixVersions field.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: No summary comment file exists in the outputs directory. The outputs contain only impact-map.md and task-1 through task-9 markdown files. There is no file representing the summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c). The impact-map.md does not contain any reference to priority, fixVersion, or propagated fields.
